topics.title: Europe deals with record June heat
topics.summary: A major heatwave is pushing parts of Europe into record June temperatures, disrupting transport and raising health concerns in countries including the UK, France, Spain, Italy and Belgium.
topics.discussionPrompt: It feels like summers are getting harder to plan around. Would you rather deal with harsh winters, or with more frequent extreme heat days?

topics.title: UN inspectors may visit Iran nuclear sites
topics.summary: The UN nuclear agency says inspectors are expected to visit Iranian nuclear sites under an interim Iran-US deal, while Tehran says details depend on a final agreement and sanctions action.
topics.discussionPrompt: International deals often come down to trust. Do inspections make tense situations feel safer, or do they just give everyone something new to argue about?

topics.title: Canadian markets slip on global caution
topics.summary: Canada's main stock index edged lower as energy and mining shares weighed on the market, with investors watching US-Iran talks and broader caution around commodities and risk.
topics.discussionPrompt: It is interesting how faraway diplomacy can nudge local markets. Do you follow the markets closely, or only notice when prices affect everyday life?

topics.title: Soccer tech exhibit opens in Vancouver
topics.summary: A FIFA Museum soccer technology exhibit has opened at Science World in Vancouver, showing how data, broadcasting, refereeing tools and innovation shape the modern game ahead of the World Cup.
topics.discussionPrompt: Sports are getting more high-tech all the time. Do you think technology makes games fairer and more fun, or does it take away some of the human charm?
